summary: Refuse parent → done state transition when any child is still open
description:
src/state.ts already detects parent tickets (lines 54 and 133 check
relationships.children.length > 0) but does not enforce that all children
are in a terminal state before letting the parent transition to done.

Today only the branch guard catches half-baked parent closes — and only
when no commit on the branch contains 'todo:<parent>'. If someone happens
to have such a commit (e.g., the 'plan: N subtasks' commit on the branch),
the parent could close with open children. A state-machine rule closes
that gap.

Fix: in applyTransition for targetState='done', when isParent is true,
read each child via readTicket(repoRoot, childId) and throw if any are
NOT in TERMINAL_STATES. Use the existing TERMINAL_STATES export from
ticket.ts. Error message should name the still-open children so the
operator knows what to finish.

Done contract: tests/state.test.ts gains coverage for (a) parent close
fails when ≥1 child is open/active/blocked, (b) parent close succeeds
when every child is terminal, (c) no regression on single-ticket close.
Error message lists open child ids.

Related: f33e500d (branch guard for the same case) — this is the
belt-and-suspenders layer.

resolution.test_function: parent ticket: refuses when at least one child is still open
resolution.note: validateTransition enforces all-children-terminal for parent close. Error message lists open child ids with their state. Missing child files also block. 5 new tests, 192 passing.
